rationale: The determinism check requires `r1.0 == r2.0` where r1.0 : ID = EndPoint { id: Vec<u8> }. The function's postcondition only constrains `id@ == self_@[k]` (Seq<u8> view equality, equivalent to AbstractEndPoint equality), not `id` itself. In Verus, Vec<T> does not have an extensional axiom that view-equal implies value-equal (confirmed by minimal test: `requires v1@ == v2@, ensures v1 == v2` fails postcondition; `=~=` also fails; broadcast use group_vec_axioms does not include such an axiom). Thus from `r1.0@ == r2.0@` we cannot derive `r1.0 == r2.0` without adding a forbidden `assume(...)`. The GLB component `r1.1@ == r2.1@` is provable (uniqueness of greatest_lower_bound_spec via the gap clause, by case analysis on k1 vs k2 under cmp connected/transitivity), but the EndPoint component is not. Tried (1) bogus assert (previous attempt), (2) `K::cmp_properties()`-only, (3) full GLB-uniqueness proof; the EndPoint equality remains the blocker.
